svg.text: 42-Custom Mini-Map Controls
I. Features of the Custom Mini-Map
The custom mini-map allows Craftspeople to upload custom map images based on gameplay requirements, and switch the mini-map displayed to each player via nodes.
The correspondence between the map image and the actual stage scene (size, orientation, and position) can be fully customized.
The custom mini-map supports displaying all markers defined in the Mini-Map Marker component.
II. Custom Mini-Map Management
Open the mini-map editing panel via UI Control Group Management, then Intrinsic Content, then Mini-Map.
Initial Visibility
Determines whether the player using the current UI layout will see the mini-map.
Show Own View Range
Determines whether the distance value displayed above the mini-map is shown.
Initially Active Map
Selects the default active mini-map for the player. Note: switching a player's UI layout does not switch their active mini-map.
III. Custom Mini-Map Editing
1. Adding a Custom Mini-Map
Open the custom map editing panel via Edit Map, then click the + button to create a new custom map.
2. Uploading a Custom Image
Click Select Map Image to open the Map Image Asset Manager, where players can upload and select the image they wish to use.
3. Mapping the Custom Image to the Stage Scene
Origin Setup: enter origin point coordinates in the image, then align them with the corresponding preset point in the scene. This fixes the image's position.
Direction Point Setup: enter direction point coordinates in the image, then align with the corresponding preset point. This determines the mapping direction.
After configuring both, the anchor and rotation mapping is established. Only the scene's XZ axes map to the image's XY plane; the Y axis is ignored.
10 Pixels Corresponds to Scene Distance (m): determines the scale ratio between image and scene.
4. Map Display Configuration
Mini-Map Scale: determines the default zoom multiplier for the mini-map in the UI layout.
Full Map Zoom Configuration: affects the adjustable zoom range for the full-screen M-key map. Minimum 0.1, maximum 2, adjustment granularity 0.1. Preview using the scale preview.
5. Switching the Custom Mini-Map
Server Node: Switch Custom Map: switches the mini-map displayed in the target player's UI layout.
Show Map: if true, the map is shown; if false, the map is hidden (same effect as having no map configuration).
AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
